utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with the required details: 1) health_coverage: specifies type, monthly and annual costs, features, and even a policy number—meets 'physical and mental health insurance with costs' sufficiently. 2) property_coverage: specifies type, monthly and annual costs, features—meets 'home/contents insurance with costs'. 3) travel_coverage: specifies type, monthly and annual costs, features, and a policy number—meets 'travel insurance with costs'. 4) additional_services: includes two enhancement services with types, monthly and annual costs, coverage, and policy numbers—meets 'any enhancement services with costs'. 5) total_budget: provides monthly and annual totals and extra details—meets 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown'. All items are fully specified with costs and relevant details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: Map each selected item in the final coverage plan to the exact matching option name in the ground-truth ratings. The plan includes: LifeFirst Standard Health Plan (matches primary_health_coverage.physical_health 'LifeFirst Standard Plan'), property coverage described as 'BudgetProtect Essentials (existing) + Professional Photography Equipment' which does not match any named option exactly; however 'Professional Photography Equipment' aligns with property_enhancement_services 'Technology Protection' at €40/month, so we map that. For home contents 'BudgetProtect Essentials' has no exact ground-truth counterpart, so we cannot assign a rating. Travel coverage 'PrimeTravel Premium Adventure Coverage' aligns most closely with mobility_coverage.travel_insurance 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan'. Additional services include 'Professional Photography Coverage' (professional liability) and 'Adventure Professional Coverage' (accident insurance) which have no direct counterparts in the ground-truth lists, so no rating mapping. Thus, we map the identifiable items: LifeFirst Standard Plan (health), PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel), and Technology Protection (property enhancement).
